agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"An existing production logistics system enforces a strict one-carrier-per-shipment invariant with supervisor-only reassignment. The product team has requested an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be automatically divided among multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of freight to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The transport provider assigned to handle a Shipment."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment","definition":"The locked binding of exactly one Carrier to a Shipment. Once set, only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds the weight threshold."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic decomposition of an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different Carrier without Supervisor approval."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is the only authority permitted to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment is sent out; the system guarantees every Shipment has exactly one Carrier at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency and state integrity guarantee enforced at dispatch. If violated, the system cannot determine who is responsible for a shipment, creating liability and tracking failures.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in the system, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization policy — management could decide to expand reassignment rights to other roles. It protects against unauthorized changes but is not a physical or data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to reassign a Carrier without Supervisor credentials must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment must have exactly one Carrier assigned.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the core dispatch invariant — without it, freight leaves with no accountable party, breaking chain of custody, insurance, and regulatory compliance.","testable_assertion":"No Shipment can transition to 'dispatched' status unless it has exactly one assigned Carrier."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Shipment weighing over 500 kg is created","when":"The auto-split feature triggers","then":"The original Shipment is decomposed into N sub-shipments, each of which individually satisfies BR-1 (exactly one Carrier assigned per sub-shipment)."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Shipment is split into sub-shipments","when":"Any sub-shipment reaches dispatch","then":"That sub-shipment has exactly one Carrier, preserving the dispatch invariant (BR-3)."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A Shipment weighing 500 kg or less","when":"The Shipment is processed","then":"No auto-split occurs; the existing single-carrier assignment flow applies unchanged."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An auto-split has occurred creating sub-shipments","when":"A user attempts to reassign a Carrier on any sub-shipment","then":"The reassignment is rejected unless performed by a Supervisor (BR-2 still applies to each sub-shipment)."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Shipment is auto-split into sub-shipments","when":"The parent Shipment is queried","then":"The system provides full traceability: the parent Shipment references all child sub-shipments, and each child references the parent."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A Shipment weighs exactly 500 kg","expected_behavior":"No split occurs — the threshold is 'exceeds 500 kg', so 500 kg exactly is handled by the standard single-carrier flow."},{"scenario":"A Shipment weighs 1500 kg and must be split into 3 sub-shipments, but only 2 carriers are available","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — the system must define whether to block the split until carriers are available, partially split, or queue for assignment. This needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A Carrier is reassigned on a sub-shipment by a Supervisor after auto-split","expected_behavior":"Allowed — Supervisor authority still applies per BR-2. The sub-shipment is an independent Shipment for assignment purposes."},{"scenario":"A parent Shipment has already been assigned a Carrier, then its weight is updated to exceed 500 kg","expected_behavior":"Contradicts BR-2: the existing carrier assignment is locked. Auto-splitting would require breaking that lock without Supervisor involvement."},{"scenario":"Sub-shipment weights do not divide evenly (e.g., 750 kg shipment — two 375 kg sub-shipments or three 250 kg?)","expected_behavior":"Splitting logic is undefined. The product request does not specify how weight is distributed among sub-shipments."},{"scenario":"One sub-shipment fails delivery but others succeed","expected_behavior":"The parent Shipment's overall status is ambiguous — is it partially delivered, failed, or pending? Lifecycle of parent vs. children needs definition."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"If implemented incorrectly, splitting shipments could create carrier billing disputes (who pays for what portion), insurance gaps on sub-shipments, and potential revenue leakage from incorrect weight-based pricing.","operational":"Introduces significant complexity to dispatch, tracking, and delivery confirmation workflows. Operations teams must now manage parent-child shipment relationships, reconcile partial deliveries, and handle split-specific exceptions.","user":"Customers who track a single shipment number will now see it fragment into multiple sub-shipments. Without clear UX, this creates confusion about delivery status and accountability.","risk":"HIGH — the feature as stated directly violates two production invariants. Shipping it without resolving the contradictions risks data corruption (shipments with zero or multiple carriers), regulatory non-compliance (broken chain of custody), and operational chaos.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must handle a new parent-child shipment model, reconcile split deliveries, and manage increased complexity in carrier coordination."},{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Their exclusive authority over carrier reassignment is bypassed by the auto-split mechanism, undermining their control function."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"May receive partial shipments they did not originally agree to handle. Carrier contracts and SLAs may not cover auto-assigned sub-shipments."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"A single shipment number now maps to multiple deliveries, complicating tracking, billing, and claims."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Must reconcile charges across multiple carriers for what was originally a single shipment. Pricing models may not support sub-shipment billing."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal","impact":"Chain of custody documentation must account for split shipments. Insurance coverage per sub-shipment needs verification."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The feature request directly contradicts two established production domain rules (one-carrier invariant and supervisor-only reassignment). Until the product team clarifies whether these invariants should be relaxed, redefined, or whether 'sub-shipment' is a new aggregate that sidesteps them, the spec cannot be implemented without breaking the existing domain model. The low confidence reflects the severity of the contradictions, not analysis quality.","high_confidence_areas":["The one-carrier-per-shipment invariant is clearly established and in production","The supervisor-only reassignment rule is clearly established","The auto-split feature as described violates both rules","Sub-shipments as independent Shipments could preserve BR-1 if the domain model is explicitly extended"],"low_confidence_areas":["How sub-shipment weight distribution should work","Parent-child lifecycle management (status rollup, partial delivery)","Whether carrier availability constraints apply at split time","Billing and pricing model for split shipments"],"assumptions":["The existing domain rules as stated are accurate and currently enforced in production","The 500 kg threshold is a hard business rule, not a configurable parameter","Sub-shipments would be modeled as first-class Shipment entities in the domain"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature requires assigning MULTIPLE carriers to what is logically a single shipment, directly violating BR-1 ('a Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier'). Even if sub-shipments are introduced as a new concept, the product request says the original shipment is 'handled by a different carrier' (plural), which contradicts the one-carrier invariant. The domain model must be explicitly extended to define whether a sub-shipment IS a Shipment (preserving BR-1 per sub-shipment) or whether BR-1 is being relaxed.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Supervisors","Compliance","Engineering"],"options":["A: Introduce 'Sub-shipment' as a first-class Shipment entity — each sub-shipment gets exactly one Carrier, preserving BR-1. The parent Shipment becomes a logical grouping with no direct Carrier assignment.","B: Relax BR-1 to allow multiple Carriers per Shipment for split scenarios, fundamentally changing the domain model.","C: Reject the feature as incompatible with the current domain model and propose an alternative (e.g., manual pre-split by Supervisors before dispatch)."],"recommendation":"Option A — it preserves the one-carrier invariant at the sub-shipment level while enabling the business capability. However, this requires explicitly modeling the parent-child relationship and defining lifecycle rules."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature bypasses Supervisor involvement for carrier assignment, directly violating BR-2 ('only a Supervisor can reassign'). Auto-split implicitly performs carrier assignment on newly created sub-shipments without Supervisor approval. The product team must clarify whether auto-split constitutes a 'new assignment' (exempt from BR-2) or a 'reassignment' (requiring Supervisor override).","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Operations","Compliance"],"options":["A: Define auto-split carrier assignment as 'initial assignment' on new sub-shipment entities, not 'reassignment' — thus BR-2 does not apply because no prior assignment existed on the sub-shipment.","B: Require Supervisor approval for the split operation itself, even if individual sub-shipment assignments are automated.","C: Create a new 'System' actor with assignment privileges specifically for auto-split scenarios."],"recommendation":"Option A — if sub-shipments are new entities, their first carrier assignment is not a reassignment and BR-2 is preserved. The Supervisor lock still applies to any subsequent changes to sub-shipment carrier assignments."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers to a single shipment without redefining the domain model","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier"},{"violation":"Auto-split performs carrier assignment without Supervisor involvement","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a Shipment"}]}

The product team has requested an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be automatically divided among multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipme
